%% solve(String) -> String
solve(S) ->
    % 1. Identify vowels and their original positions
    Vowels = [<<"a">>, <<"e">>, <<"i">>, <<"o">>, <<"u">>],
    VowelPositions = find_vowel_positions(S, Vowels),
    
    % 2. Calculate frequencies

    % Get unique vowels and their first appearance index
    FirstAppearance = get_first_appearance(S, Vowels),
    
    % Prepare tuples for sorting: {Frequency, FirstIndex, VowelChar}
    SortableVowels = 
        lists:map(fun(V) ->
            {lists:nth(V, Frequencies), FirstAppearance[V], V}
        end, Vowels),
        
    % Custom comparison function for sorting
    compare_vowels = fun(T1, T2) ->
        {Freq1, Index1, V1} = T1,
        {Freq2, Index2, V2} = T2,
        
        % Compare frequencies (descending: Freq2 vs Freq1)
        case compare(Freq2, Freq1) of
            ok -> ok;
            eq -> % Frequencies are equal, compare by first index (ascending: Index1 vs Index2)

                      eq -> % Indices are equal (shouldn't happen for unique vowels, but for safety)
                            compare(V1, V2);
                      lt -> lt;
                      gt -> gt
                  end;

            gt -> gt
        end

    SortedVowels = lists:sort(compare_vowels, SortableVowels),
    
    % Extract the sorted list of vowel characters
    SortedVowelChars = lists:map(fun({_, _, V}) -> V end, SortedVowels),
    
    % 4. Reconstruct the string
    ReconstructedString = insert_sorted_vowels(S, VowelPositions, SortedVowelChars),

            end
        else

        end

            _ ->

        case binary:sublist(Char, 0, 1) of
